metadata.summary: End-to-end testing with Playwright and Cypress: real browser automation, POM architecture, and CI/CD integration.

content.title: E2E Testing
content.summary: Guides writing maintainable E2E tests using the Page Object Model, handling authentication, API mocking, visual regression, and sharded CI pipelines. Covers Playwright (primary) and Cypress (secondary).

why.p: E2E tests validate full user journeys across real browsers. Without structure (POM, fixtures, data factories), suites become brittle and slow. This methodology enforces patterns that reduce flakiness and keep CI times bounded.

list.item: Writing or reviewing Playwright / Cypress test suites
Setting up E2E infrastructure from scratch (config, auth, CI sharding)
Debugging flaky tests or selector failures
Unit or integration tests (no browser needed) → use unit-testing or testing-pytest
API-only testing → use HTTP client directly
OAuth flows with real external providers (use storageState workaround instead)

section.name: test-pyramid
section.rule: Follow the 70/20/10 pyramid: 70% unit, 20% integration, 10% E2E.
section.rationale: E2E tests are the most expensive to maintain and slowest to run. Reserve them for critical user journeys only.
section.rule: Each E2E test must cover a complete user journey, not an individual UI component.

example.title: E2E test covers the checkout flow end-to-end
E2E test checks a single button's CSS — use a unit/component test instead

section.name: page-object-model
section.rule: Every page or major feature section must have a corresponding Page Object class.
Page Objects expose actions and assertions, not raw selectors.
Page Objects must not contain test assertions — return values instead.

example.title: LoginPage with POM pattern

section.name: selector-strategy
section.rule: Prefer role-based selectors: getByRole, getByLabel, getByPlaceholder.
Use data-testid only when semantic selectors are not feasible.
Never use CSS class selectors — they change with refactors.
Never use XPath in Playwright tests.

section.name: project-layout
section.rule: Co-locate POM files with tests: tests/e2e/pages/, tests/e2e/fixtures/, tests/e2e/specs/.
Place shared fixtures and auth setup in tests/e2e/fixtures/.

section.name: authentication-with-storagestate
section.rule: Never log in through the UI for every test — use storageState to reuse authenticated sessions.
Create a global auth setup that runs once and saves storageState to a file.

example.title: storageState auth setup

section.note: For OAuth providers, use API-based token injection instead of UI login — UI OAuth flows change frequently and cannot be reliably automated.

section.name: api-mocking-with-route
section.rule: Mock external APIs with page.route() to isolate E2E tests from third-party services.
Mock only external boundaries — test your own API with real calls.

example.title: Payment error on gateway failure

section.name: data-factories
section.rule: Use factory functions with faker to generate test data — never hardcode user emails or IDs.
Factories must be deterministic within a test run but vary between runs.

example.title: User data factory with faker

section.name: visual-regression
section.rule: Use toHaveScreenshot() for components, not full pages — full-page snapshots break on dynamic content.
Always mask dynamic regions (timestamps, avatars) before taking screenshots.

section.name: mobile-testing
section.rule: Use Playwright device descriptors, not viewport resizing, to test mobile layouts.

example.title: Mobile device projects in Playwright

section.name: flakiness-prevention
section.rule: Never use fixed sleep() — use page.waitForSelector(), expect().toBeVisible(), or waitForURL().
Never rely on network timing — mock external APIs or use waitForResponse().
Avoid test interdependence — each test must set up its own state.
Use test.describe.configure({ mode: 'parallel' }) only when tests are fully isolated.

antipattern.description: Fixed sleep causes flakiness across different CI machine speeds
antipattern.reason: Replace with: await expect(page.getByText('Success')).toBeVisible() — Playwright waits automatically.

section.name: retry-strategy
section.rule: Set retries: 2 in CI, retries: 0 in local dev to surface failures quickly.
Enable trace: 'on-first-retry' to capture failure context without storing traces for every run.

example.title: Playwright config with retries and traces

section.name: ci-sharding
section.rule: Shard large suites across multiple CI machines to keep total wall time under 10 minutes.
Use --shard=N/total with merge-reports to combine results.

example.title: GitHub Actions matrix sharding
example.code: # GitHub Actions matrix sharding strategy: matrix: shard: [1, 2, 3, 4] steps: - run: npx playwright test --shard=${{ matrix.shard }}/4 - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 with: name: blob-report-${{ matrix.shard }} path: blob-report/ retention-days: 1

section.name: trace-artifacts
section.rule: Always upload Playwright reports as CI artifacts for debugging failures.
Merge blob reports before publishing the HTML report when using sharding.

template.purpose: Full Playwright config: projects, sharding, reporter, retries
Abstract base Page class with navigation helpers
Data factory with faker, builder pattern
